standard: AOS/APS — The Open Standard for Agent-Preferred Brands

axes.name: Agent Preference Score
axes.question: Why should an agent choose this brand?

requirements.title: llms.txt
requirements.detail: Serve /llms.txt: H1, blockquote summary, H2 sections, markdown links to key resources AND to proof objects (case studies).

requirements.title: llms-full.txt
requirements.detail: Serve /llms-full.txt: the full canonical corpus in one file.

requirements.title: AGENTS.md
requirements.detail: Serve /AGENTS.md with when-to-use guidance for agents.

requirements.title: robots.txt + sitemap
requirements.detail: robots.txt with an explicit AI-crawler policy; sitemap.xml (and sitemap.md).

requirements.title: JSON-LD identity
requirements.detail: schema.org Organization (with contactPoint) and Service/Product as applicable.

requirements.title: Proof objects as structured data
requirements.detail: Each case study emitted as schema.org CreativeWork/CaseStudy (client, sector, result, metric, date, verification basis).

requirements.title: Markdown content negotiation
requirements.detail: Return a markdown mirror of any page when the request sends Accept: text/markdown.

requirements.title: A2A Agent Card
requirements.detail: Serve /.well-known/agent-card.json describing identity, endpoints, and skills.

requirements.title: Agent permissions
requirements.detail: Serve /.well-known/agent-permissions.json; brand.json boundaries SHOULD be surfaced here.

requirements.title: MCP Server Card
requirements.detail: Serve /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json (OAuth Protected Resource metadata) when tools are exposed.

requirements.title: NLWeb /ask endpoint
requirements.detail: Expose a natural-language /ask endpoint over the brand's knowledge (retrieval, not generation).

requirements.title: brand.json Claims & Proofs
requirements.detail: Serve /.well-known/brand.json with claims[] and proofs[] per spec/claims-proofs.md. Each claim carries a mandatory boundary. AOS never fabricates evidence.

requirements.title: Mandatory boundary
requirements.detail: Every claim declares applicable_for and not_applicable_for. A missing not_applicable_for is an explicit marker, never omitted. Boundaries raise trust.

requirements.title: Proofs link to claims
requirements.detail: Each proof carries verifiable_by (public_url | third_party_platform | signed_client | internal) and links to the claim it supports; both sides synchronized. AOS links proofs, it does not create them.

requirements.title: Derived confidence
requirements.detail: confidence is derived from sample size n (n<2 -> null), never self-assigned. No fabricated metrics.

requirements.title: Ed25519 signature
requirements.detail: Sign the exact bytes of brand.json; serve the detached signature at /.well-known/brand.json.sig.

requirements.title: Public key discovery
requirements.detail: Publish the Ed25519 public key at /.well-known/keys.json so any agent can verify without trusting the issuer.

hard_rules: No fabricated evidence: missing data emits an explicit marker or empty array.
confidence is derived from sample size, never self-assigned.
boundary is mandatory and is a positive trust signal.
No third-party auditor is claimed unless one exists (signed_client != signed_independent).
